title: Route does not always recalculate following deviation
description: Upon deviating from the planned route, I expect a recalculation in no more than 30 seconds, preferably less. The app does not do so reliably.

title: Changes to route do not update Glass quickly
description:
I stopped my route on the phone app and selected a new one, and it took about one minute to get a new instruction on my Glass.

A new route or recalculation should immediately push to the Glass.
close_reason: Stop button now keeps RideService + BT transport alive; selecting a new route reuses the existing connection via RideService.startRoute() instead of paying for a Bluetooth reconnect. Also tracks currentRouteId so stopRide() can send a matching RouteEnd(CANCELLED) to Glass.

title: Glass app disconnects during use
description:
During a ride, sometimes the Glass app disconnects from the phone app even though the two are still Bluetooth paired. It does not reconnect, and it is not clear to the user that there is a problem.

Ideally, the app would never disconnect. Investiate and fix sources of connection instability.

IF the Glass app does lose its connection, it should try to reestablish the connection. If it cannot, it should wake up the screen and show a "DISCONNECTED" message in large text until dismissed.

It is acceptable to modify both apps to resolve this issue.
close_reason: Added application-level Keepalive (5s ping, 15s watchdog) to RFCOMM + TCP transports on both sides; closes the socket on silent BT drops so the existing reconnect path runs. On Glass, transport disconnect now triggers a 10s-grace screen-wake alert with large 'DISCONNECTED' text on the LiveCard, cleared on reconnect. 6 new Keepalive unit tests + existing TcpTransport roundtrip pass.

title: Map orientation toggle is not visible
description: The map orientation toggle created in glass-nav-uou is not shown on my phone.

title: Provide information on starting a new route
description:
Upon starting a new route, inform the user of the initial direction to travel and speak it on the Glass.

Additionally display the remaining distance and time on both phone and Glass. Provide a gear icon settings button on the phone to let the user choose what it shown on each of phone and Glass.
close_reason: Implemented: (1) initial-direction TTS on route start via PacketDispatcher.maybeSpeakInitialDirection; (2) remaining distance + ETA computed in RideService and sent as new Packet.Progress fields; (3) DisplayPrefs + 4-slot DisplaySettingsDialogFragment with gear icon in fragment_ride_controls.xml; (4) phone renders per phoneTop/phoneBottom prefs; (5) Packet.DisplayConfig (0x05) pushed to Glass on connect and on settings change.

title: Improve visibility
description: Increase the size and/or contrast of roads on both maps. Increase the size and contrast ofthe path. Change the position marker to an arrow representing the actual direction of travel, or if that is undetermined, the intended direction.
close_reason: Increased road/path contrast and size; replaced position dot with directional arrow.

title: Use speed-based pre-turn wakeups
description:
There is currently a wakeup 150m before the next turn. That's about right for cycling on average, but too close if cycling fast, and not ideal for driving or walking. Instead, there should be a minimum, plus extra distance based on current speed. Change this to:

Walking: 30m
Cycling: 150m
Driving: 500m

Or 0.7m per km/h, whichever is greater.

title: Toggle to orinet map in the direction of travel
description: Add a setting in the phone app to switch between north-up and travel-direction-up. This should apply to the map sent to the Glass as well.

title: Map is cluttered
description:
The map shown on both the phone and Glass displays contains a large amount of clutter, making it difficulty to recognize the roads and route at a glance.

Investigate options to declutter the map. If there's a straightforward obvious option, implement it. If not, create a follow up issue with options.

Increase the contrast of the map if possible.
close_reason: Switched mapsforge render theme from OSMARENDER to BIKER and added an XmlRenderThemeMenuCallback whitelisting only nature/road_detail/off_road. Hides POIs, house numbers, contour lines, transit/parking markers, and other categorized rules; roads/water/terrain (uncategorized) still render. Centralized in new MapTheme.kt, applied to both SnippetRenderer (Glass HUD) and RoutePreviewFragment (phone). Contrast improves as a side effect of removing visual noise — explicit contrast tuning deferred (would require either userScaleFactor bump or post-render ColorMatrix; both worth considering after a real-bike legibility test).
